Score Summary: This panel breaks down your speech into 9 core metrics across Content, Delivery, and Simplicity. Every metric in the green ('just right') awards you 1 point toward your final Grade.
Time: Your total speaking time. Speaking for 60-80 seconds is 1p, 20-59 is 0.5p, and going under 20s or over 90s cancels your Grade entirely.
Grade: Your final score out of 10. Metrics in the green give 1 point, yellow gives 0.75, and red gives 0.25 points.
Content: Measures the substance of your speech. Aim for 30%+ Personal examples and 30%+ Visual descriptions.
Delivery: Measures how you sound. Aim for 100-150 Words Per Minute, 3 to 5 Syllables/sec (to avoid mumbling), and pause for at least 400 milliseconds often.
Simplicity: Measures how easy your speech is to understand. Keep your average sentence between 5 to 15 words.
Personal: Measures storytelling and personal examples. Best speakers talk about a specific person doing specific things about 30-60% of the time.
Visual: Measures words that paint a picture (objects, colors, sizes). Aim for 20-50%.
Intangible: Content that is not Personal or Visual (abstract concepts and filler). Aim for under 30%.
Words/min: Your speaking pace. 100-150 words per minute is 'just right'.
Syllable/sec: Our 'Mumble Meter'. It measures how densely packed your syllables are during active speech. 3 to 5 syllables per second is ideal.
Pause %: The percentage of time you spend silent. Aim for 10-30%.
Words/Sent: The average length of your sentences. Aim for 5-15 words.
Comp. Grade: The reading grade level of your speech. A grade of 5-10 is 'just right' and plain english.
Simple %: The percentage of your words within the 10,000 words used by people learning english. 85-95% is ideal.
In 3sec chunks, counts how varied is your pause lengths. Varrying pause lengths affects meaning.
In 3sec chunks, counts how varied is your speaking length & volume. Varying volume & speaking length is engaging.
In 3sec chunks, counts your slow and fast sentences. Varying rhythm & cadence is engaging.
